features.description: Flurry of Blows: expend 1 Focus Point for three Unarmed Strikes instead of two. Patient Defense: gain Temporary Hit Points equal to two Martial Arts die rolls. Step of the Wind: move a willing creature within 5 feet (Large or smaller) with you.

features.name: Self-Restoration
features.description: Remove Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned condition from yourself at end of each turn. Forgoing food and drink doesn't give Exhaustion.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Feature gained at level 11 based on Monk subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Deflect Energy
features.description: Now use Deflect Attacks against any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

features.name: Disciplined Survivor
features.description: Proficiency in all saving throws. When you fail a saving throw, expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it.

features.name: Perfect Focus
features.description: When you roll initiative and don't use Uncanny Metabolism, regain expended Focus Points until you have 4 if you have 3 or fewer.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Feature gained at level 17 based on Monk subclass.

features.name: Superior Defense
features.description: At start of your turn, expend 3 Focus Points for Resistance to all damage except Force for 1 minute or until Incapacitated.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of Irresistible Offense is recommended.

features.name: Body and Mind
features.description: Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

majors.name: Warrior of Mercy
majors.subtitle: Manipulate Forces of Life and Death
majors.description: Warriors of Mercy manipulate the life force of others. These Monks are wandering physicians who bring a swift end to their enemies. They often wear masks, presenting themselves as faceless bringers of life and death.

features.name: Hand of Harm
features.description: Once per turn when you hit with Unarmed Strike and deal damage, expend 1 Focus Point for extra Necrotic damage (Martial Arts die + Wisdom modifier).

features.name: Hand of Healing
features.description: Magic action, expend 1 Focus Point to touch a creature and restore Hit Points (Martial Arts die + Wisdom modifier). When using Flurry of Blows, replace one Unarmed Strike with Hand of Healing without expending Focus Point.

features.name: Implements of Mercy
features.description: Gain proficiency in Insight and Medicine skills and Herbalism Kit.

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description: Hand of Harm can also give Poisoned condition. Hand of Healing can also end one condition: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned.

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description: When using Flurry of Blows, replace each Unarmed Strike with Hand of Healing without expending Focus Points. When making Unarmed Strike with Flurry and dealing damage, use Hand of Harm without expending Focus Point. Uses equal to Wisdom modifier (minimum once).

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description: Magic action, touch corpse of creature that died within 24 hours, expend 5 Focus Points. Creature returns to life with 4d10 + Wisdom modifier Hit Points. Conditions removed: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, Stunned.

majors.name: Warrior of Shadow
majors.subtitle: Harness Shadow Power for Stealth and Subterfuge
majors.description: Warriors of Shadow practice stealth and subterfuge, harnessing the power of the Shadowfell.

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: Darkness - expend 1 Focus Point to cast Darkness spell (you can see within it, can move area 60 feet per turn). Darkvision 60 feet (or +60 feet if already have). Shadowy Figments - know Minor Illusion spell (Wisdom spellcasting ability).

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: While entirely within Dim Light or Darkness, Bonus Action teleport up to 60 feet to unoccupied space in Dim Light or Darkness. Advantage on next melee attack.

features.name: Improved Shadow Step
features.description: Expend 1 Focus Point to remove Dim Light/Darkness requirement. Make Unarmed Strike after teleporting.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: Magic action in Dim Light or Darkness, expend 3 Focus Points for 1 minute. Invisibility. Partially Incorporeal - move through occupied spaces. Shadow Flurry - use Flurry of Blows without expending Focus Points.

majors.name: Warrior of the Elements
majors.subtitle: Wield Strikes and Bursts of Elemental Power
majors.description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes.

features.name: Elemental Attunement
features.description: Expend 1 Focus Point at start of turn to imbue yourself with elemental energy for 10 minutes. Reach +10 feet. Elemental Strikes - choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage. Force Strength save or move target up to 10 feet.

features.name: Manipulate Elements
features.description: Know Elementalism spell (Wisdom spellcasting ability).

features.name: Elemental Burst
features.description: Magic action, expend 2 Focus Points, 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on point within 120 feet. Choose damage type. Dexterity save or take damage equal to three Martial Arts die. Half on success.

features.name: Stride of the Elements
features.description: While Elemental Attunement active, gain Fly Speed and Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

features.name: Elemental Epitome
features.description: While Elemental Attunement active: Damage Resistance to one elemental type (change each turn). Destructive Stride - Step of the Wind Speed +20, creatures take Martial Arts die damage when you enter 5 feet. Empowered Strikes - once per turn, extra Martial Arts die damage on Unarmed Strike.

majors.name: Warrior of the Open Hand
majors.subtitle: Master Unarmed Combat Techniques
majors.description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: When you hit with attack from Flurry of Blows, impose one effect: Addle (no Opportunity Attacks), Push (Strength save or pushed 15 feet), Topple (Dexterity save or Prone).

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: Bonus Action to roll Martial Arts die, regain Hit Points equal to number rolled + Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). Uses equal to Wisdom modifier (minimum once).

features.name: Fleet Step
features.description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: When you hit with Unarmed Strike, expend 4 Focus Points to set up imperceptible vibrations lasting Monk level days. End them as Attack action or take Attack action and forgo one attack. Target makes Constitution save: 10d12 Force damage (half on success). Only one creature at a time.

multiclassing.core_traits_gained: Gain the Hit Point Die trait. Gain the Monk's level 1 features.

name: Paladin
class_description:
Paladins are united by their oaths to stand against the forces of annihilation and corruption.

Whether sworn before a god's altar, in a sacred glade before nature spirits, or in a moment of desperation and grief with the dead as the only witnesses, a Paladin's oath is a powerful bond. It is a source of power that turns a devout warrior into a blessed champion. Paladins train to learn the skills of combat, mastering a variety of weapons and armor. Even so, their martial skills are secondary to the magical power they wield: power to heal the injured, smite their foes, and protect the helpless and those who fight at their side. Almost by definition, the life of a Paladin is an adventuring life, for every Paladin lives on the front lines of the cosmic struggle against annihilation.

core_traits.skill_proficiencies: Choose 2: Athletics, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Persuasion, or Religion
core_traits.armor_training: Light, Medium, and Heavy armor and Shields
core_traits.starting_equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Chain Mail, Shield, Longsword, 6 Javelins, Holy Symbol, Priest's Pack, and 9 GP; or (B) 150 GP

features.name: Lay On Hands
features.description: Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you finish a Long Rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of Hit Points equal to five times your Paladin level. As a Bonus Action, you can touch a creature and draw power from the pool to restore Hit Points up to the maximum remaining. You can also expend 5 Hit Points from the pool to remove the Poisoned condition from the creature.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. Spell Slots: Half-caster progression. Level 1-3: 2 slots. Level 4-5: 3 slots. Level 6-7: 4 slots. Level 8-9: 4+2 slots. Level 10-11: 4+3 slots. Level 12-13: 4+3+2 slots. Level 14-15: 4+3+3 slots. Level 16-17: 4+3+3+1 slots. Level 18-19: 4+3+3+2 slots. Level 20: 4+3+3+3 slots. Prepared Spells: Start with 2 level 1 spells. Increases with Paladin level. Spellcasting Ability: Charisma. Spellcasting Focus: Holy Symbol. Changing Your Prepared Spells: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one spell on your list with another Paladin spell.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Alternatively, you can choose Blessed Warrior: You learn two Cleric cantrips of your choice (Guidance and Sacred Flame recommended). Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them.

features.name: Paladin's Smite
features.description: You always have the Divine Smite spell prepared. You can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes. You start with one effect: Divine Sense. Divine Sense: As a Bonus Action, open your awareness to detect Celestials, Fiends, and Undead for 10 minutes or until Incapacitated. Know location and creature type of any such creature within 60 feet. Also detect consecrated/desecrated places/objects. You can use Channel Divinity twice. Regain one use on Short Rest, all on Long Rest. Additional use at level 11. Save DC = spell save DC from Spellcasting feature.

features.name: Paladin Subclass
features.description: You gain a Paladin subclass of your choice. The Oath of Devotion, Oath of Glory, Oath of the Ancients, and Oath of Vengeance subclasses.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Faithful Steed
features.description: You always have the Find Steed spell prepared. You can also cast the spell once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description: You radiate a protective aura in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you. The aura is inactive while you have the Incapacitated condition. You and your allies in the aura gain a bonus to saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum +1). If another Paladin is present, a creature can benefit from only one Aura of Protection at a time.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Gained at level 7 based on Paladin Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Abjure Foes
features.description: As a Magic action, expend one use of Channel Divinity. Target creatures equal to Charisma modifier (minimum 1) within 60 feet. Each makes Wisdom save or has Frightened condition for 1 minute or until taking damage. While Frightened, can only do one of: move, take action, or take Bonus Action.

features.name: Aura of Courage
features.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the Frightened condition while in your Aura of Protection.

features.name: Radiant Strikes
features.description: Your strikes now carry supernatural power. When you hit a target with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or Unarmed Strike, the target takes extra 1d8 Radiant damage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Gained at level 13 based on Paladin Subclass.

features.name: Restoring Touch
features.description: When you use Lay On Hands on a creature, you can also remove one or more conditions: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned. Must expend 5 Hit Points from the healing pool for each condition.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Gained at level 15 based on Paladin Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Gained at level 17 based on Paladin Subclass.

features.name: Aura Expansion
features.description: Your Aura of Protection is now a 30-foot Emanation.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of Truesight is recommended.

features.name: (capstone - depends on subclass)
features.description: Gained at level 20 based on Paladin Subclass.

majors.name: Oath of Devotion
majors.subtitle: Uphold the Ideals of Justice and Order
majors.description: Tenets: Let your word be your promise. Protect the weak and never fear to act. Let your honorable deeds be an example.

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: Expend one use of Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon with positive energy for 10 minutes. Add Charisma modifier to attack rolls (minimum +1). Hit deals normal damage type or Radiant damage. Weapon emits Bright Light in 20-foot radius and Dim Light 20 feet beyond.

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and your allies have Immunity to Charmed condition while in Aura of Protection.

features.name: Smite of Protection
features.description: Whenever you cast Divine Smite, you and your allies have Half Cover while in your Aura of Protection until start of next turn.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: Bonus Action to imbue Aura of Protection with holy power for 10 minutes. Holy Ward: Advantage on saving throws by Fiends/Undead. Radiant Damage: enemies in aura take Radiant damage (Charisma modifier + Proficiency Bonus) when they start turn. Sunlight: aura filled with Bright Light that is sunlight.

majors.name: Oath of Glory
majors.subtitle: Strive for the Heights of Heroism
majors.description: Tenets: Endeavor to be known by your deeds. Face hardships with courage. Inspire others to strive for glory.

features.name: Inspiring Smite
features.description: Immediately after casting Divine Smite, expend Channel Divinity and distribute Temporary Hit Points (2d8 + Paladin level) among creatures within 30 feet.

features.name: Peerless Athlete
features.description: Bonus Action, expend Channel Divinity. For 1 hour: Advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, Long/High Jump distance +10 feet.

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: Your Speed +10 feet. Allies entering Aura of Protection or starting turn there get +10 feet Speed until end of their next turn.

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: When you or ally within 10 feet is hit by attack, take Reaction to grant bonus to AC (Charisma modifier, minimum +1). If attack misses, make one weapon attack against attacker. Uses = Charisma modifier (minimum once).

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: Bonus Action for 10 minutes. Charismatic: Advantage on all Charisma checks. Saving Throw Reroll: Reaction to reroll failed save. Unerring Strike: once per turn, cause missed weapon attack to hit.

majors.name: Oath of the Ancients
majors.subtitle: Preserve Life and Light in the World
majors.description: Tenets: Kindle the light of hope. Shelter life. Delight in art and laughter.

features.name: Nature's Wrath
features.description: Magic action, expend Channel Divinity. Creatures within 15 feet make Strength save or be Restrained for 1 minute (repeats save each turn).

features.name: Aura of Warding
features.description: You and allies have Resistance to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage while in Aura of Protection.

features.name: Undying Sentinel
features.description: When reduced to 0 HP but not killed, drop to 1 HP and regain 3x Paladin level Hit Points. Can't be aged magically, cease visibly aging. Once per Long Rest.

features.name: Elder Champion
features.description: Bonus Action for 1 minute. Diminish Defiance: enemies in aura have Disadvantage on saves against your spells/Channel Divinity. Regeneration: regain 10 HP at start of each turn. Swift Spells: cast action spells as Bonus Action.

majors.name: Oath of Vengeance
majors.subtitle: Punish Evildoers at Any Cost
majors.description: Tenets: Show the wicked no mercy. Fight injustice and its causes. Aid those harmed by injustice.

features.name: Vow of Enmity
features.description: Take Attack action, expend Channel Divinity to utter vow against creature within 30 feet. Advantage on attack rolls against the creature for 1 minute or until used again. Transfer to different creature if target drops to 0 HP.

features.name: Relentless Avenger
features.description: When you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, reduce creature's Speed to 0 until end of current turn. Move up to half Speed as part of same Reaction.

features.name: Soul of Vengeance
features.description: Immediately after a creature under Vow of Enmity hits or misses with an attack, take Reaction to make melee attack against that creature if within range.

features.name: Avenging Angel
features.description: Bonus Action for 10 minutes. Flight: spectral wings, Fly Speed 60 feet, hover. Frightful Aura: enemies in Aura of Protection make Wisdom save or be Frightened for 1 minute or until taking damage. Attack rolls against Frightened creature have Advantage.

multiclassing.core_traits_gained: Gain Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields.

name: Ranger
class_description:
Away from bustling cities, amid the trees of trackless forests and across wide plains, rangers keep their unending watch in the wilderness.

Rangers learn to track their quarry as a predator does, moving stealthily through the wilds and hiding themselves in brush and rubble. Thanks to their deep connection with nature, Rangers can also cast spells that harness primal powers of the wilderness. A Ranger's talents and magic are honed with deadly focus to protect the world from the ravages of monsters and tyrants.

core_traits.skill_proficiencies: Choose 3: Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Investigation, Nature, Perception, Stealth, or Survival
core_traits.armor_training: Light and Medium armor and Shields
core_traits.starting_equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Studded Leather Armor, Scimitar, Shortsword, Longbow, 20 Arrows, Quiver, Druidic Focus (sprig of mistletoe), Explorer's Pack, and 7 GP; or (B) 50 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to channel the magical essence of nature to cast spells. Spell Slots. The Ranger Features table shows how many spell slots you have. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest. Half-caster progression. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells. To start, choose two level 1 Ranger spells. Cure Wounds and Ensnaring Strike are recommended. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one spell on your list with another Ranger spell. Spellcasting Ability. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Ranger spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use a Druidic Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Ranger spells.

features.name: Favored Enemy
features.description: You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared. You can cast it twice without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. The number of times you can cast the spell without a spell slot increases when you reach certain Ranger levels, as shown in the Favored Enemy column of the Ranger Features table.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose.

features.name: Deft Explorer
features.description: Expertise. Choose one of your skill proficiencies with which you lack Expertise. You gain Expertise in that skill. Languages. You know two languages of your choice from the language tables.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Alternatively, you can choose Druidic Warrior: You learn two Druid cantrips of your choice (Guidance and Starry Wisp recommended). Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them.

features.name: Ranger Subclass
features.description: You gain a Ranger subclass of your choice. The Beast Master, Fey Wanderer, Gloom Stalker, and Hunter subclasses.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Roving
features.description: Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You also have a Climb Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Depends on subclass selection.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: Choose two of your skill proficiencies with which you lack Expertise. You gain Expertise in those skills.

features.name: Tireless
features.description: Temporary Hit Points. As a Magic action, you can give yourself Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d8 + Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). Uses = Wisdom modifier (minimum once). Regain all on Long Rest. Decrease Exhaustion. Whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level decreases by 1.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Depends on subclass selection.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Relentless Hunter
features.description: Taking damage can't break your Concentration on Hunter's Mark.

features.name: Nature's Veil
features.description: You invoke spirits of nature to magically hide yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can give yourself the Invisible condition until end of your next turn. Uses = Wisdom modifier (minimum once). Regain all on Long Rest.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Depends on subclass selection.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Precise Hunter
features.description: You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your Hunter's Mark.

features.name: Feral Senses
features.description: Your connection to the forces of nature grants you Blindsight with a range of 30 feet.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of Dimensional Travel is recommended.

features.name: Foe Slayer
features.description: The damage die of your Hunter's Mark is a d10 rather than a d6.

majors.name: Beast Master
majors.subtitle: Bond with a Primal Beast
majors.description: A Beast Master forms a mystical bond with a special animal, drawing on primal magic and a deep connection to the natural world.

features.name: Primal Companion
features.description: Magically summon a primal beast. Choose stat block: Beast of the Land (Medium, AC 13+Wis, HP 5+5xRanger level, Speed 40 ft climb, Beast's Strike 1d8+2+Wis Bludgeoning/Piercing/Slashing), Beast of the Sea (Medium, AC 13+Wis, HP 5+5xRanger level, Speed 5 ft swim 60 ft, Beast's Strike 1d6+2+Wis Bludgeoning/Piercing + Grappled), or Beast of the Sky (Small, AC 13+Wis, HP 4+4xRanger level, Speed 10 ft fly 60 ft, Beast's Strike 1d4+3+Wis Slashing). The beast is Friendly to you and your allies. In combat, acts during your turn. Dodge action unless you take Bonus Action to command it. You can sacrifice one of your attacks to command Beast's Strike. Restoring: Magic action, touch dead beast (within 1 hour), expend spell slot, beast returns after 1 minute with all HP restored.

features.name: Exceptional Training
features.description: When commanding Primal Companion to take an action, also command it to take Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action using its Bonus Action. It can deal Force damage or its normal damage type.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: When commanding Primal Companion to take Beast's Strike, the beast can use it twice. First time each turn it hits a creature under Hunter's Mark, deals extra Force damage equal to the bonus damage of that spell.

features.name: Share Spells
features.description: When you cast a spell targeting yourself, you can also affect your Primal Companion beast if within 30 feet.

majors.name: Fey Wanderer
majors.subtitle: Wield Fey Might and Fury
majors.description: A fey mystique surrounds you, thanks to the boon of an archfey or a location in the Feywild. Your joyful laughter brightens the hearts of the downtrodden, and your martial prowess strikes terror in your foes.

features.name: Dreadful Strikes
features.description: When you hit with a weapon, deal extra 1d4 Psychic damage (increases to 1d6 at Ranger level 11). Extra damage can only be taken once per turn.

features.name: Otherworldly Glamour
features.description: Bonus to Charisma checks = Wisdom modifier (minimum +1). Gain proficiency in Deception, Performance, or Persuasion.

features.name: Beguiling Twist
features.description: Advantage on saving throws to avoid/end Charmed or Frightened. When you or creature within 120 feet succeeds on save vs Charmed/Frightened, take Reaction to force different creature within 120 feet to make Wisdom save (your spell save DC) or be Charmed/Frightened for 1 minute.

features.name: Fey Reinforcements
features.description: Cast Summon Fey without Material component. Cast it once without spell slot, regain on Long Rest. Can modify it to not require Concentration (duration becomes 1 minute).

features.name: Misty Wanderer
features.description: Cast Misty Step without spell slot, uses = Wisdom modifier (minimum once). When casting Misty Step, bring one willing creature within 5 feet to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of your destination.

majors.name: Gloom Stalker
majors.subtitle: Draw on Shadow Magic to Fight Your Foes
majors.description: Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places, wielding magic drawn from the Shadowfell to combat enemies that lurk in darkness.

features.name: Dread Ambush
features.description: Ambusher's Leap: at start of first turn of each combat, Speed +10 until end of that turn. Dreadful Strike: when you hit with a weapon, deal extra 2d6 Psychic damage (uses = Wisdom modifier, minimum once, regain on Long Rest). Initiative Bonus: add Wisdom modifier to Initiative roll.

features.name: Umbral Sight
features.description: Darkvision 60 feet (or +60 feet if already have). While entirely in Darkness, have Invisible condition to any creature that relies on Darkvision.

features.name: Iron Mind
features.description: Proficiency in Wisdom saving throws (or Intelligence or Charisma if already have).

features.name: Stalker's Flurry
features.description: Dreadful Strike Psychic damage becomes 2d8. When using Dreadful Strike, can cause: Sudden Strike (make another attack against different creature within 5 feet of original target), or Mass Fear (target + creatures within 10 feet make Wisdom save or be Frightened until start of next turn).

features.name: Shadowy Dodge
features.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, take Reaction to impose Disadvantage. Whether hit or miss, teleport up to 30 feet to unoccupied space you can see.

majors.name: Hunter
majors.subtitle: Protect Nature and People from Destruction
majors.description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect nature and people from forces that would destroy them.

features.name: Hunter's Lore
features.description: While a creature is marked by Hunter's Mark, know whether it has Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: Choose one, can replace on Short/Long Rest: Colossus Slayer (extra 1d8 damage to creature missing HP, once per turn), or Horde Breaker (once per turn, make another attack with same weapon against different creature within 5 feet of original target and within weapon's range).

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: Choose one, can replace on Short/Long Rest: Escape the Horde (Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you), or Multiattack Defense (when a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn).

features.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
features.description: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by Hunter's Mark, also deal that spell's extra damage to a different creature within 30 feet of the first creature.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: When you take damage, take Reaction to give yourself Resistance to that damage and any other damage of the same type until end of current turn.

multiclassing.core_traits_gained: Gain Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons, proficiency in one skill of your choice from the Ranger's skill list, and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields.

name: Rogue
class_description:
Rogues rely on cunning, stealth, and their foes' vulnerabilities to get the upper hand in any situation.

They have a knack for finding the solution to just about any problem. A few even learn magical tricks to supplement their other abilities. Many Rogues focus on stealth and deception, while others refine skills that help them in a dungeon environment, such as climbing, finding and disarming traps, and opening locks. In combat, Rogues prioritize subtle strikes over brute strength. They would rather make one precise strike than wear an opponent down with a barrage of blows. Some Rogues began their careers as criminals, while others used their cunning to fight crime. Whatever a Rogue's relation to the law, no common criminal or officer of the law can match the subtle brilliance of the greatest Rogues.

core_traits.skill_proficiencies: Choose 4: Acrobatics, Athletics, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Perception, Persuasion, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth
core_traits.weapon_proficiencies: Simple weapons and Martial weapons that have the Finesse or Light property
core_traits.starting_equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Leather Armor, 2 Daggers, Shortsword, Shortbow, 20 Arrows, Quiver, Thieves' Tools, Burglar's Pack, and 8 GP; or (B) 100 GP

proficiencies: Martial weapons with Finesse or Light property

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Sleight of Hand and Stealth are recommended if you have proficiency in them. At Rogue level 6, you gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Sneak Attack
features.description: You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or a Ranged weapon. The extra damage's type is the same as the weapon's type. You don't need Advantage on the attack roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target, the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition, and you don't have Disadvantage on the attack roll. The extra damage increases as you gain Rogue levels.

features.name: Thieves' Cant
features.description: You know Thieves' Cant and one other language of your choice.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose.

features.name: Cunning Action
features.description: On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a Bonus Action: Dash, Disengage, or Hide.

features.name: Rogue Subclass
features.description: You gain a Rogue subclass of your choice. The Arcane Trickster, Assassin, Soulknife, and Thief subclasses.

features.name: Steady Aim
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

features.name: Cunning Strike
features.description: When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one of the following Cunning Strike effects (each has a die cost - number of Sneak Attack damage dice you must forgo): Poison (Cost: 1d6). Add toxin, target makes Constitution save or has Poisoned condition for 1 minute (repeats save each turn). Must have Poisoner's Kit. Trip (Cost: 1d6). Large or smaller target makes Dexterity save or has Prone condition. Withdraw (Cost: 1d6). Immediately after attack, move up to half Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you (round down).

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you take no damage if you succeed and only half damage if you fail. Can't use if Incapacitated.

features.name: Reliable Talent
features.description: Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of your skill or tool proficiencies, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

features.name: Improved Cunning Strike
features.description: You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage, paying the die cost for each effect.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature

features.name: Devious Strikes
features.description: New Cunning Strike options: Daze (Cost: 2d6). Target makes Constitution save or on next turn can only do one of: move or take action or Bonus Action. Knock Out (Cost: 6d6). Target makes Constitution save or has Unconscious condition for 1 minute (repeats save at end of each turn). Obscure (Cost: 3d6). Target makes Dexterity save or has Blinded condition until end of its next turn.

features.name: Slippery Mind
features.description: You gain proficiency in Wisdom and Charisma saving throws.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature

features.name: Elusive
features.description: No attack roll can have Advantage against you unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of the Night Spirit is recommended.

features.name: Stroke of Luck
features.description: If you fail a D20 Test, you can turn the roll into a 20. Once per Short or Long Rest.

majors.name: Arcane Trickster
majors.subtitle: Enhance Stealth with Arcane Spells
majors.description: Some Rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with spells, learning magical tricks to aid them in their trade.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You know three cantrips: Mage Hand and two other cantrips from Wizard list (Mind Sliver and Minor Illusion recommended). Replace one cantrip (except Mage Hand) on level up. Level 10: learn another Wizard cantrip. Spell Slots (half-caster): Level 3: 2 (2 1st). Level 4-5: 3 (3 1st). Level 6-7: 4 (4 1st). Level 8-9: 4+2 (4 1st, 2 2nd). Level 10-11: 4+3 (4 1st, 3 2nd). Level 12-13: 4+3+2 (4 1st, 3 2nd, 2 3rd). Level 14-15: 4+3+3 (4 1st, 3 2nd, 3 3rd). Level 16-17: 4+3+3+1 (4 1st, 3 2nd, 3 3rd, 1 4th). Level 18-19: 4+3+3+2 (4 1st, 3 2nd, 3 3rd, 2 4th). Level 20: 4+3+3+3 (4 1st, 3 2nd, 3 3rd, 3 4th). Prepared Spells: Start with 3 level 1 Wizard spells. Increases with Rogue level. Spellcasting Ability: Intelligence. Spellcasting Focus: Arcane Focus.

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: When you cast Mage Hand, cast as Bonus Action, make spectral hand Invisible. Control as Bonus Action, make Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks through it.

features.name: Magical Ambush
features.description: If you have Invisible condition when you cast a spell on a creature, it has Disadvantage on saving throw against the spell on the same turn.

features.name: Versatile Trickster
features.description: When you use Trip option of Cunning Strike on a creature, you can also use it on another creature within 5 feet of the spectral hand.

features.name: Spell Thief
features.description: Immediately after a creature casts a spell targeting you or including you in its area, take Reaction to force Intelligence save (your spell save DC). On failed save, negate spell and steal knowledge of spell (level 1+, of a level you can cast). For 8 hours you have the spell prepared. Creature can't cast it until 8 hours pass. Once per Long Rest.

majors.name: Assassin
majors.subtitle: Practice the Grim Art of Death
majors.description: An Assassin's training focuses on using stealth, poison, and disguise to eliminate foes with deadly efficiency.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: Initiative: Advantage on Initiative rolls. Surprising Strikes: During first round of each combat, Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn. If Sneak Attack hits any target during that round, target takes extra damage equal to your Rogue level.

features.name: Assassin's Tools
features.description: Gain Disguise Kit and Poisoner's Kit, proficiency with them.

features.name: Infiltration Expertise
features.description: Masterful Mimicry: unerringly mimic another person's speech, handwriting, or both if studied for 1 hour. Roving Aim: Speed not reduced to 0 by Steady Aim.

features.name: Envenom Weapons
features.description: When using Poison option of Cunning Strike, target also takes 2d6 Poison damage on failed save. This damage ignores Resistance to Poison damage.

features.name: Death Strike
features.description: When you hit with Sneak Attack on first round of combat, target makes Constitution save (DC 8 + Dex mod + Prof Bonus) or attack's damage is doubled.

majors.name: Soulknife
majors.subtitle: Strike Foes with Psionic Blades
majors.description: A Soulknife strikes with the mind, cutting through barriers both physical and psychic.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: Psionic Energy Dice: Level 3: d6 x4. Level 5: d8 x6. Level 9: d8 x8. Level 11: d10 x8. Level 13: d10 x10. Level 17: d12 x12. Psi-Bolstered Knack: If you fail an ability check using skill/tool with proficiency, roll one Psionic Energy Die and add to check. Expended only if roll then succeeds. Psychic Whispers: Magic action, choose creatures up to Proficiency Bonus, roll one Psionic Energy Die. For hours equal to number rolled, chosen creatures can speak telepathically with you (within 35 feet). First use after Long Rest doesn't expend die.

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: Manifest shimmering blades of psychic energy. When you take Attack action or make Opportunity Attack, manifest Psychic Blade in free hand. Simple Melee, 1d6 Psychic + ability modifier, Finesse, Thrown (60/120), Mastery: Vex (doesn't count against Weapon Mastery). Vanishes after hit/miss. After attacking, can make melee or ranged attack with second psychic blade as Bonus Action (1d4 damage).

features.name: Soul Blades
features.description: Homing Strikes: If attack with Psychic Blade misses, roll Psionic Energy Die and add to attack roll. If causes hit, die expended. Psychic Teleportation: Bonus Action, manifest Psychic Blade, expend Psionic Energy Die, throw to unoccupied space up to 10x die roll feet. Teleport to that space, blade vanishes.

features.name: Psychic Veil
features.description: Magic action, gain Invisible condition for 1 hour. Ends after dealing damage or forcing saving throw. Once per Long Rest (or expend Psionic Energy Die to restore).

features.name: Rend Mind
features.description: When you use Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage, force target to make Wisdom save (DC 8 + Dex mod + Prof Bonus). On fail, target has Stunned condition for 1 minute (repeats save at end of each turn). Once per Long Rest (or expend 3 Psionic Energy Dice to restore).

majors.name: Thief
majors.subtitle: Hunt for Treasure as a Classic Adventurer
majors.description: A mix of burglar, treasure hunter, and explorer, you are the epitome of an adventurer.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: Bonus Action: Sleight of Hand (pick lock/disarm trap with Thieves' Tools or pick pocket), or Use an Object (Utilize action or Magic action to use magic item).

features.name: Second-Storywork
features.description: Climber: Climb Speed equal to Speed. Jumper: determine jump distance using Dexterity instead of Strength.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: Stealth Attack (Cost: 1d6). If you have Hide action's Invisible condition, this attack doesn't end that condition on you if you end turn behind Three-Quarters Cover or Total Cover.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: Attunement: attune to up to 4 magic items at once. Charges: when using magic item property that expends charges, roll 1d6, on 6 use without expending. Scrolls: use any Spell Scroll, Intelligence as spellcasting ability. Cantrip/level 1: cast reliably. Higher level: succeed on Intelligence (Arcana) check (DC 10 + spell level) to cast. On fail, scroll disintegrates.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: Take two turns during first round of any combat. First turn at normal initiative, second turn at initiative minus 10.

multiclassing.core_traits_gained: Gain Hit Point Die, proficiency in one skill of your choice from the Rogue's skill list, proficiency with Thieves' Tools, and training with Light armor.

name: Sorcerer
class_description:
Sorcereers wield innate magic that is stamped into their being.

Some Sorcerers can't name the origin of their power, while others trace it to strange events in their personal or family history. The blessing of a dragon or a diviner at a baby's birth or the strike of lightning from a clear sky might spark a Sorcerer's gift. So too might the gift of a deity, exposure to the strange magic of another plane of existence, or a glimpse into the inner workings of reality. Whatever the origin, the result is an indelible mark on the Sorcerer, a churning magic that can be passed down through generations. Sorcerers don't learn magic; the raw, roiling power of magic is part of them. The essential art of a Sorcerer is learning to harness and channel that innate magic, allowing the Sorcerer to discover new and startling ways to unleash their power. As Sorcerers master their innate magic, they grow more attuned to its origin, developing distinct powers that reflect its source. Sorcerers are rare. Some family lines produce exactly one Sorcerer in every generation, but most of the time, the talents of sorcery appear as a fluke. People who have this magical power soon discover that it doesn't like to stay quiet. A Sorcerer's magic wants to be wielded.

core_traits.skill_proficiencies: Choose 2: Arcana, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Persuasion, or Religion
core_traits.starting_equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Spear, 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (crystal), Dungeoneer's Pack, and 28 GP; or (B) 50 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Drawing from your innate magic, you can cast spells. Cantrips. You know four Sorcerer cantrips of your choice. Light, Prestidigitation, Shocking Grasp, and Sorcerous Burst are recommended. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another Sorcerer cantrip. When you reach Sorcerer levels 4 and 10, you learn another Sorcerer cantrip. Spell Slots. Full caster progression. Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells. To start, choose two level 1 Sorcerer spells. Burning Hands and Detect Magic are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Sorcerer levels. Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Sorcerer spell. Spellcasting Ability. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Sorcerer spells. Spellcasting Focus. You can use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Sorcerer spells.

features.name: Innate Sorcery
features.description: An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain the following benefits: The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1. You have Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast. You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Font of Magic
features.description: You can tap into the wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by Sorcery Points. You have 2 Sorcery Points, gaining more at higher levels. Converting Spell Slots to Sorcery Points. You can expend a spell slot to gain Sorcery Points equal to the slot's level (no action required). Creating Spell Slots. As a Bonus Action, transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot. Cost: 1st=2 SP, 2nd=3 SP, 3rd=4 SP, 4th=5 SP, 5th=6 SP. Minimum levels: 2, 3, 5, 7, 9. Any spell slot created with this feature vanishes when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You gain two Metamagic options of your choice. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it unless otherwise noted. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one option. You gain two more options at level 10 and two more at level 17.

features.name: Sorcerer Subclass
features.description: You gain a Sorcerer subclass of your choice. The Aberrant Sorcery, Clockwork Sorcery, Draconic Sorcery, and Wild Magic Sorcery subclasses.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Sorcerous Restoration
features.description: When you finish a Short Rest, you can regain expended Sorcery Points, but no more than a number equal to half your Sorcerer level (round down). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Sorcery Incarnate
features.description: If you have no uses of Innate Sorcery left, you can use it if you spend 2 Sorcery Points when you take the Bonus Action to activate it. In addition, while your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use up to two of your Metamagic options on each spell you cast.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature at level 10.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature at level 14.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature at level 17.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of Dimensional Travel is recommended.

features.name: Arcane Apotheosis
features.description: While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use one Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Sorcery Points on it.

majors.name: Aberrant Sorcery
majors.subtitle: Wield Unnatural Psionic Power
majors.description: An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you.

features.name: Psionic Spells
features.description: Level 3: Arms of Hadar, Calm Emotions, Detect Thoughts, Dissonant Whispers, Mind Sliver. Level 5: Hunger of Hadar, Sending. Level 7: Evard's Black Tentacles, Summon Aberration. Level 9: Rary's Telepathic Bond, Telekinesis.

features.name: Telepathic Speech
features.description: Bonus Action, choose one creature within 30 feet. Communicate telepathically while within a number of miles equal to Charisma modifier (minimum 1 mile). Duration: minutes equal to Sorcerer level.

features.name: Psionic Sorcery
features.description: When you cast any level 1+ spell from Psionic Spells feature, cast by expending spell slot normally or spending Sorcery Points equal to spell's level. If cast using Sorcery Points, no Verbal or Somatic components, no Material components unless consumed or have cost.

features.name: Psychic Defenses
features.description: Resistance to Psychic damage. Advantage on saving throws to avoid/end Charmed or Frightened condition.

features.name: Revelation in Flesh
features.description: Bonus Action, spend 1+ Sorcery Points to magically alter body for 10 minutes. For each SP spent, gain one benefit: Aquatic Adaptation (Swim Speed = 2x Speed, breathe underwater), Glistening Flight (Fly Speed = Speed, hover), See the Invisible (see Invisible creatures within 60 feet), Wormhole Movement (move through spaces as narrow as 1 inch, escape nonmagical restraints).

features.name: Warping Implosion
features.description: Magic action, teleport to unoccupied space within 120 feet. Each creature within 30 feet of space you left makes Strength save or takes 3d10 Force damage and pulled toward space you left. Once per Long Rest (or spend 5 SP to restore).

majors.name: Clockwork Sorcery
majors.subtitle: Channel Cosmic Forces of Order
majors.description: The cosmic force of order has suffused you with magic. That power arises from Mechanus or a realm like it—a plane of existence shaped entirely by clockwork efficiency.

features.name: Clockwork Spells
features.description: Level 3: Aid, Alarm, Lesser Restoration, Protection from Evil and Good. Level 5: Dispel Magic, Protection from Energy. Level 7: Freedom of Movement, Summon Construct. Level 9: Greater Restoration, Wall of Force. Manifestations of Order (1d6): 1=Spectral cogwheels hover behind you. 2=Hands of clock spin in your eyes. 3=Skin glows with brassy sheen. 4=Floating equations overlay body. 5=Spellcasting Focus becomes Tiny clockwork mechanism. 6=Ticking of gears/ringing clock heard.

features.name: Restore Balance
features.description: When a creature you can see within 60 feet is about to roll a d20 with Advantage or Disadvantage, take Reaction to prevent roll from being affected. Uses = Charisma modifier (minimum once). Regain on Long Rest.

features.name: Bastion of Law
features.description: Magic action, expend 1-5 Sorcery Points to create magical ward around self or creature within 30 feet. Ward represented by number of d8s equal to SP spent. When warded creature takes damage, can expend dice, roll them, reduce damage by total rolled. Ward lasts until Long Rest or use again.

features.name: Transe of Order
features.description: Bonus Action for 1 minute. Attack rolls against you can't benefit from Advantage. Whenever you make a D20 Test, treat roll of 9 or lower as 10. Once per Long Rest (or spend 5 SP to restore).

features.name: Clockwork Cavalcade
features.description: Magic action, summon spirits in 30-foot Cube. Effects: Heal (up to 100 HP divided among creatures), Repair (damaged objects repaired instantly), Dispel (every spell of level 6 and lower ends). Once per Long Rest (or spend 7 SP to restore).

majors.name: Draconic Sorcery
majors.subtitle: Breathe the Magic of Dragons
majors.description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death bequeathed some of its magical power to you or your ancestor.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: Hit Point maximum increases by 3, and increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level. Parts covered by dragon-like scales. While not wearing armor, base AC = 10 + Dexterity + Charisma modifiers.

features.name: Draconic Spells
features.description: Level 3: Alter Self, Chromatic Orb, Command, Dragon's Breath. Level 5: Fear, Fly. Level 7: Arcane Eye, Charm Monster. Level 9: Legend Lore, Summon Dragon.

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: Choose one damage type (Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison). Resistance to that type. When you cast a spell that deals damage of that type, add Charisma modifier to one damage roll.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: Bonus Action, cause draconic wings to appear for 1 hour. Fly Speed 60 feet. Once per Long Rest (or spend 3 SP to restore).

features.name: Dragon Companion
features.description: Cast Summon Dragon without Material component. Cast it once without spell slot, regain on Long Rest. Can modify so it doesn't require Concentration (duration becomes 1 minute).

majors.name: Wild Magic Sorcery
majors.subtitle: Unleash Chaotic Magic
majors.description: Your innate magic stems from the forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. Whatever its source, this magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description: Once per turn, you can roll 1d20 immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot. If you roll a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table. If the magical effect is a spell, it is too wild to be affected by your Metamagic.

features.name: Feats of Chaos
features.description: Manipulate chaos to give yourself Advantage on one D20 Test before rolling. Must cast Sorcerer spell with spell slot or finish Long Rest before using again. If you cast Sorcerer spell with spell slot before Long Rest, automatically roll on Wild Magic Surge table.

features.name: Bend Fate
features.description: Immediately after another creature you can see rolls the d20 for a D20 Test, take Reaction and spend 1 Sorcery Point to roll 1d4 and apply as bonus or penalty (your choice) to the d20 roll.

features.name: Controlled Chaos
features.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you can roll twice and use either number.

features.name: Tamed Surge
features.description: Immediately after casting Sorcerer spell with spell slot, create effect of your choice from Wild Magic Surge table instead of rolling. Can choose any effect except final row. Once per Long Rest.

multiclassing.core_traits_gained: Gain the Hit Point Die from the Core Sorcerer Traits table.

name: Warlock
class_description:
Warlock's quest for knowledge that lies hidden in the fabric of the multiverse.

They often begin their search for magical power by delving into tomes of forbidden lore, dabbling in invocations meant to attract the power of extraplanar beings, or seeking places of power where the influence of these beings can be felt. In no time, each Warlock is drawn into a binding pact with a powerful patron. Drawing on the ancient knowledge of beings such as angels, archfey, demons, devils, hags, and alien entities of the Far Realm, Warlocks piece together arcane secrets to bolster their own power. Warlocks view their patrons as resources, as means to the end of achieving magical power. Some Warlocks respect, revere, or even love their patrons; some serve their patrons grudgingly; and some seek to undermine their patrons even as they wield the power their patrons have given them. Once a pact is made, a Warlock's thirst for knowledge and power can't be slaked with mere study. Most Warlocks spend their days pursuing greater power and deeper knowledge, which typically means some kind of adventure.

core_traits.skill_proficiencies: Choose 2: Arcana, Deception, History, Intimidation, Investigation, Nature, or Religion
core_traits.starting_equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Leather Armor, Sickle, 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (orb), Book (occult lore), Scholar's Pack and 15 GP; or (B) 100 GP

features.name: Eldritch Invocations
features.description: You have unearthed Eldritch Invocations, pieces of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with an abiding magical ability or other lessons. You gain one invocation of your choice. Prerequisites: If an invocation has a prerequisite, you must meet it to learn that invocation. Replacing and Gaining Invocations: Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your invocations with another one for which you qualify. You can't replace an invocation if it's a prerequisite for another invocation that you have. When you gain certain Warlock levels, you gain more invocations of your choice, as shown in the Warlock Features table. You can't pick the same invocation more than once unless its description says otherwise.

features.name: Pact Magic
features.description: Through occult ceremony, you have formed a pact with a mysterious entity to gain magical powers. Cantrips: You know two Warlock cantrips of your choice. Eldritch Blast and Prestidigitation are recommended. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your cantrips. When you reach Warlock levels 4 and 10, you learn another Warlock cantrip. Spell Slots: You have spell slots to cast your Warlock spells of levels 1-5. All slots are the same level. You regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells. To start, choose two level 1 Warlock spells. Charm Person and Hex are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Warlock levels. The chosen spells must be of a level no higher than the Slot Level for your level. Changing Your Prepared Spells: Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Warlock spell of an eligible level. Spellcasting Ability: Charisma is the spellcasting ability for your Warlock spells. Spellcasting Focus: You can use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Warlock spells.

features.name: Magical Cunning
features.description: You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of it, you regain expended Pact Magic spell slots but no more than a number equal to half your maximum (round up). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Warlock Subclass
features.description: You gain a Warlock subclass of your choice. The Archfey Patron, Celestial Patron, Fiend Patron, and Great Old One Patron subclasses.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Contact Patron
features.description: You always have the Contact Other Plane spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast the spell without expending a spell slot to contact your patron, and you automatically succeed on the spell's saving throw. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Subclass feature

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 6 spell)
features.description: Your patron grants you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one level 6 Warlock spell as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You gain another Warlock spell of your choice that can be cast in this way when you reach Warlock levels 13 (level 7 spell), 15 (level 8 spell), and 17 (level 9 spell). You regain all uses of your Mystic Arcanum when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your arcanum spells with another Warlock spell of the same level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 7 spell)
features.description: Your patron grants you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one level 7 Warlock spell as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You regain all uses of your Mystic Arcanum when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your arcanum spells with another Warlock spell of the same level.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 8 spell)
features.description: Your patron grants you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one level 8 Warlock spell as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You regain all uses of your Mystic Arcanum when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your arcanum spells with another Warlock spell of the same level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 9 spell)
features.description: Your patron grants you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one level 9 Warlock spell as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You regain all uses of your Mystic Arcanum when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your arcanum spells with another Warlock spell of the same level.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of Fate is recommended.

features.name: Eldritch Master
features.description: When you use your Magical Cunning feature, you regain all your expended Pact Magic spell slots.

majors.name: Archfey Patron
majors.subtitle: Bargain with Whimsical Fey
majors.description: Your pact draws on the power of the Feywild. When you choose this subclass, you might make a deal with an archfey, such as the Prince of Frost; the Queen of Air and Darkness, ruler of the Gloaming Court; Titania of the Summer Court; or an ancient hag. Or you might call on a spectrum of Fey, weaving a web of favors and debts. Whoever they are, your patron is often inscrutable and whimsical.

features.name: Steps of the Fey
features.description: Cast Misty Step without spell slot, uses = Charisma modifier (minimum once), regain on Long Rest. Additional effects: Refreshing Step (gain 1d10 Temporary Hit Points after teleport), Taunting Step (creatures within 5 feet of space you left make Wisdom save or have Disadvantage on attack rolls against others).

features.name: Misty Escape
features.description: Cast Misty Step as Reaction in response to taking damage. Additional effects: Disappearing Step (have Invisible condition until start of next turn or after attacking/dealing damage/casting spell), Dreadful Step (creatures within 5 feet of space you left or appear in make Wisdom save or take 2d10 Psychic damage).

features.name: Beguiling Defenses
features.description: Immune to Charmed condition. After a creature hits you with an attack roll, take Reaction to reduce damage by half and force attacker to make Wisdom save or take Psychic damage equal to damage you take. Once per Long Rest (or expend Pact Magic spell slot to restore).

features.name: Bewitching Magic
features.description: Immediately after casting an Enchantment or Illusion spell using an action and a spell slot, cast Misty Step as part of the same action without expending a spell slot.

majors.name: Celestial Patron
majors.subtitle: Call on the Power of the Heavens
majors.description: Your pact draws on the Upper Planes, the Realms of everlasting bliss. You might enter an agreement with an empyrean, a couatl, a sphinx, a unicorn, or another heavenly entity. Or you might call on numerous such beings as you pursue goals aligned with theirs. Your pact allows you to experience a hint of the holy light that illuminates the multiverse.

features.name: Healing Light
features.description: Pool of d6s (number = 1 + Warlock level). Bonus Action to heal yourself or creature within 60 feet, expending dice (max = Charisma modifier). Roll dice, restore HP equal to total. Regain all on Long Rest.

features.name: Radiant Soul
features.description: Resistance to Radiant damage. Once per turn, when a spell you cast deals Radiant or Fire damage, add Charisma modifier to that spell's damage against one of the spell's targets.

features.name: Celestial Resilience
features.description: Gain Temporary Hit Points equal to Warlock level + Charisma modifier when you use Magical Cunning or finish a Short or Long Rest. Up to 5 creatures you can see gain half your Warlock level + Charisma modifier Temporary Hit Points.

features.name: Searing Vengeance
features.description: When you or ally within 60 feet is about to make a Death Saving Throw, unleash radiant energy. Creature regains HP equal to half its Hit Point maximum, ends Pending condition. Each creature within 30 feet takes 2d8 + Charisma modifier Radiant damage and has Blinded condition until end of current turn. Once per Long Rest.

majors.name: Fiend Patron
majors.subtitle: Make a Deal with the Lower Planes
majors.description: Your pact draws on the Lower Planes, the realms of perdition. You might forge a bargain with a demon lord such as Demogorgon or Orcus; an archdevil such as Asmodeus; or a pit fiend, balor, yugoloth, or night hag that is especially mighty.

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, gain Temporary Hit Points equal to Charisma modifier + Warlock level (minimum 1). Also gain this benefit if someone else reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points.

features.name: Dark One's Own Look
features.description: When you make an ability check or saving throw, add 1d10 to your roll after seeing the roll but before any effects occur. Uses = Charisma modifier (minimum once). Regain on Long Rest.

features.name: Fiendish Resilience
features.description: Choose one damage type (other than Force) when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Resistance to that type until you choose a different one.

features.name: Hurl Through Hell
features.description: Once per turn when you hit with an attack roll, target makes Charisma save or disappears and takes 8d10 Psychic damage (if not a Fiend), has Incapacitated condition until end of next turn, then returns to space it previously occupied. Once per Long Rest (or expend Pact Magic spell slot to restore).

majors.name: Great Old One Patron
majors.subtitle: Unearth Forbidden Lore of Ineffable Being
majors.description: Your pact binds you to an unspeakable being from the Far Realm or an elder god—a being such as Tharizdun, the Chained God; Zargon, the Returner; Hadar, the Dark Hunger; or Great Cthulhu. Or you might involve several entities without yoking yourself to one. The motives of these beings are incomprehensible, and the Great Old One might be indifferent to your existence.

features.name: Awakened Mind
features.description: Bonus Action, choose one creature within 30 feet. Communicate telepathically while within a number of miles equal to Charisma modifier (minimum 1 mile). Duration: minutes equal to Warlock level.

features.name: Psychic Spells
features.description: When you cast a Warlock spell that deals damage, you can change its damage type to Psychic. When you cast a Warlock spell that is an Enchantment or Illusion, you can do so without Verbal or Somatic components.

features.name: Clairvoyant Combatant
features.description: When you form a telepathic bond with a creature using Awakened Mind, force that creature to make Wisdom save. On failed save, creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you, and you have Advantage on attack rolls against that creature for the duration. Once per Short or Long Rest (or expend Pact Magic spell slot to restore).

features.name: Eldritch Hex
features.description: Always have Hex spell prepared. When you cast Hex and choose an ability, the target also has Disadvantage on saving throws of the chosen ability for the duration.

features.name: Thought Shield
features.description: Your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it. Resistance to Psychic damage. Whenever a creature deals Psychic damage to you, that creature takes the same amount of damage that you take.

features.name: Create Thrall
features.description: When you cast Summon Aberration, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. Duration becomes 1 minute. When summoned, the Aberration has Temporary Hit Points equal to Warlock level + Charisma modifier. First time each turn the Aberration hits a creature under Hex, deals extra Psychic damage equal to the bonus damage of that spell.

name: Wizard
class_description:
Wizards are defined by their exhaustive study of magic's inner workings.

They cast spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, and spectacular transformations. Their magic conjures monsters from other planes of existence, glimpses the future, or forms protective barriers. Their mightiest spells change one substance into another, call meteors from the sky, or open portals to other worlds. Most Wizards share a scholarly approach to magic. They examine the theoretical underpinnings of magic, particularly the categorization of spells into schools of magic. Renowned Wizards such as Bigby, Tasha, Mordenkainen, and Yolande have built on their studies to invent iconic spells now used across the multiverse. The closest a Wizard is likely to come to an ordinary life is working as a sage or lecturer. Other Wizards sell their services as advisers, serve in military forces, or pursue lives of crime or domination. But the lure of knowledge calls even the most unadventurous Wizards from the safety of their libraries and laboratories and into crumbling ruins and lost cities. Most Wizards believe that their counterparts in ancient civilizations knew secrets of magic that have been lost to the ages, and discovering those secrets could unlock the path to a power greater than any magic available in the present age.

core_traits.skill_proficiencies: Choose 2: Arcana, History, Insight, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion
core_traits.starting_equipment: Choose A or B: (A) 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Quarterstaff), Robe, Spellbook, Scholar's Pack, and 5 GP; or (B) 55 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells. Cantrips: You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. Light, Mage Hand, and Ray of Frost are recommended. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your cantrips with another Wizard cantrip. When you reach Wizard levels 4 and 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip. Spellbook: Your wizardly apprenticeship culminated in the creation of a unique book: your spellbook. It is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or someone casting Identify. It starts with six level 1 Wizard spells of your choice. Detect Magic, Feather Fall, Mage Armor, Magic Missile, Sleep, and Thunderwave are recommended. Whenever you gain a Wizard level after 1, add two Wizard spells of choice to your spellbook. Each must be of a level for which you have spell slots. Spell Slots: The Wizard Features table shows how many spell slots you have. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest. Prepared Spells of Level 1+: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells. To do so, choose four spells from your spellbook. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Wizard levels. Changing Your Prepared Spells: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any with spells from your spellbook. Spellcasting Ability: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells. Spellcasting Focus: You can use an Arcane Focus or your spellbook as a Spellcasting Focus for your Wizard spells.

features.name: Ritual Adept
features.description: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description: You can regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Scholar
features.description: By studying magic, you also specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion. You have Expertise in the chosen skill.

features.name: Wizard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. The Abjurer, Diviner, Evoker, and Illusionist subclasses.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Memorize Spell
features.description: Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you have prepared with another level 1+ spell from the book.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Depends on subclass chosen at level 3.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Depends on subclass chosen at level 3.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: Depends on subclass chosen at level 3.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat. Again at levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Spell Mastery
features.description: You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a level 1 and a level 2 spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of an action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat. Boon of Spell Recall is recommended.

features.name: Signature Spells
features.description: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast them in this way again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

majors.name: Abjurer
majors.subtitle: Shield Companions and Banish Foes
majors.description: Your study of magic is focused on spells that block, banish, or protect—ending harmful effects, banishing evil influences, and protecting the weak.

features.name: Abjuration Savant
features.description: Choose two Wizard spells from the Abjuration school (no higher than level 2), add to spellbook for free. Whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots, add one Abjuration school spell to spellbook for free.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: When you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, create a magical ward on yourself lasting until Long Rest. Ward HP = 2x Wizard level + Intelligence modifier. Ward takes damage instead of you. Ward regains HP = 2x spell slot level when you cast Abjuration spell with spell slot, or as Bonus Action expend spell slot.

features.name: Projected Ward
features.description: When a creature you can see within 30 feet takes damage, take Reaction to cause Arcane Ward to absorb damage. If ward reduced to 0 HP, warded creature takes remaining damage.

features.name: Spell Breaker
features.description: Always have Counterspell and Dispel Magic prepared. Cast Dispel Magic as Bonus Action, add Proficiency Bonus to ability check. When you cast either with spell slot, slot isn't expended if spell fails to stop a spell.

features.name: Spell Resistance
features.description: Advantage on saving throws against spells. Resistance to damage of spells.

majors.name: Diviner
majors.subtitle: Learn the Secrets of the Multiverse
